differences.title: 1. ACCESSIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Installable from any web browser, no app store required. Works offline.
differences.rightDesc: Requires app store download and approval. Centralized distribution.

differences.title: 2. DEVELOPMENT
differences.leftDesc: Develop once, deploy everywhere. Uses web technologies like HTML, CSS, JS.
differences.rightDesc: Requires separate development for iOS (Swift/Objective-C) and Android (Kotlin/Java).

differences.title: 3. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Generally good, but may not match native app speed for intensive tasks.
differences.rightDesc: Optimized for device hardware, offering smoother animations and faster execution.

differences.title: 4. HARDWARE ACCESS
differences.leftDesc: Access to device features like camera and location is improving but still restricted.
differences.rightDesc: Direct and extensive access to all device hardware and sensors.

differences.title: 5. OFFLINE CAPABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Service workers enable offline functionality and background sync.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for offline use, data stored locally.

differences.title: 6. UPDATES
differences.leftDesc: Updates are seamless upon next access, no user intervention needed.
differences.rightDesc: Users must manually download and install updates from the app store.

differences.title: 7. DISCOVERY
differences.leftDesc: Discoverable via search engines and direct links.
differences.rightDesc: Found primarily through app store search and charts.
